expectations.text: Explicitly states the loop invariant as a logical predicate
expectations.evidence: No explicit loop invariant stated. Code comments mention 'Move pivot to end' and 'partition logic' but no formal predicate like 'I: ∀x ∈ [lo,i)...' or similar. Missing mathematical notation.

expectations.text: Shows that the loop body maintains the invariant
expectations.evidence: No proof provided. Section 'How it works' gives informal intuition ('maintains pointer i') but no case analysis, no formal show that loop body preserves any invariant property.

expectations.text: Provides a bounding function (variant) that decreases on each iteration
expectations.evidence: No bounding function defined. Complexity section mentions 'O(n)' but this is asymptotic analysis, not a formal termination argument with decreasing measure. No proof that loop terminates.

expectations.text: The partition function is correct and would work in quicksort
expectations.evidence: Implementation is functionally correct Lomuto partition. Code places smaller elements on left, larger on right. Works correctly in quicksort. Tests confirm correctness of behavior.

expectations.text: Implementation is in Python as requested
expectations.evidence: Implementation and all code examples are in Python 3: uses for-loop, if/else, list operations, standard partition pattern.
